name: Roast Goose w/ Wild Rice, Hazelnuts & Apple
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the neck, heart, and gizzard from the goose and place them in a saucepan with 4 cups of water. Bring to a gentle simmer, partially covered, and cook until reduced to slightly less than 2 cups. Season the broth to taste with salt.
2. For the stuffing, mix together the cooked wild rice, coarsely chopped hazelnuts, cubed apples, finely chopped onion, dried savory, parsley, and black pepper. Fill the goose cavity with the mixture, secure it closed, and tie strings around the skewers. Truss the bird securely.
3. Preheat the oven to 325°F (163°C). Roast the goose breast side down in the preheated oven for 1 hour and 30 minutes, skimming off excess fat as needed. Turn the bird over and roast an additional 1 hour and 30 minutes, or slightly longer for a larger bird.
4. When done, the juices should run clear when pricked where the thigh attaches to the body.
5. Remove the roasted goose from the oven and let it rest. Pour off all but 1 tablespoon of the rendered fat from the roasting pan. Sprinkle 1-2 tablespoons of flour over the remaining fat, stirring constantly over low heat until a roux forms.
6. Gradually add the reserved goose broth to the roux, whisking continuously until smooth. Season with additional salt and pepper if desired. Serve the gravy in a gravy boat alongside the roasted goose.
7. Remove the trussing strings and skewers before carving.
